response:
# Combobox with Autocomplete Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), keyboard-only users, low-vision/zoom users, mobile/touch users, voice-control users, users with cognitive load sensitivity > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA (Windows), JAWS (Windows), VoiceOver (macOS + iOS), keyboard-only, mobile screen readers (VoiceOver iOS, TalkBack Android) **Feature:** A reusable autocomplete search input that filters results into a dropdown listbox, selectable by keyboard or mouse, supporting grouped results and async API loading. **Risk Level:** High **Component Type:** Combobox (editable, with list autocomplete) --- ## Scope & Context **What is being built?** A reusable React combobox component: a text input with an associated dropdown listbox that filters/fetches options as the user types, navigable and selectable by keyboard or pointer. **What user need does it address?** Fast lookup and selection of an item (person, organization, search term) from a potentially large set, with progressive disclosure so users do not have to scan a long static list. **Who needs accessibility?** - **Screen reader users** — the single hardest constituency for this pattern. They must hear: the field's name and role, that suggestions are available, how many, the currently-highlighted option as they arrow through it, group membership, the loading state, and the empty-result state. None of this is visible to them; all of it must be programmatic. - **Keyboard-only users** — must reach the field via Tab, open/navigate/select/dismiss the list entirely from the keyboard, and never get trapped. - **Low-vision / zoom users** — need a visible focus/highlight indicator that survives 200%–400% zoom and meets contrast minimums; the dropdown must not get clipped or pushed off-screen. - **Mobile / touch users** — need a touch-tappable list with adequate target size, and the on-screen keyboard must not obscure the suggestions. - **Voice-control users** (Dragon, Voice Control) — need the field reachable by its visible label name and options tappable by their visible text. - **Cognitive-load** — predictable behavior, clear "no results" messaging, no surprise focus jumps. **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA. **Assistive technologies that must be supported:** NVDA + Firefox/Chrome, JAWS + Chrome, VoiceOver + Safari (desktop and iOS), TalkBack + Chrome (Android), keyboard-only on all desktop browsers. **Risk level — High.** Justification: this is the most failure-prone widget in the entire APG catalog. It combines (a) `aria-activedescendant` focus management — a model most engineers implement incorrectly; (b) live-region announcements with timing hazards; (c) async loading state; (d) a focus model where DOM focus and "visual/AT focus" deliberately diverge. Each is individually a common source of production a11y defects. Combined, they justify a detailed plan. **What existing code does this modify/extend?** None. New component, greenfield, built on the WAI-ARIA APG Combobox pattern. No `file:line` baseline to cite because no prior implementation exists. **Constraints:** - Must work desktop + mobile (responsive, touch, on-screen keyboard). - Results can be fetched asynchronously from an API — network latency, race conditions, and loading states are in scope. - Must support async loading explicitly (not just client-side filtering of a static array). - Built on a custom React component library (no native `` shortcut, no third-party combobox dependency assumed). **Negative space — what this plan does NOT cover:** It does not cover a *multi-select* / token / "tags" combobox (each selection produces a removable chip) — that is a different APG variant and a separate plan. It does not cover an inline-autocomplete model where the input auto-completes the remaining characters (`aria-autocomplete="both"`); this plan deliberately specifies **`aria-autocomplete="list"`** (suggestions in the list only, input text not auto-filled as you type) because it is the lowest-surprise model for screen reader users and the requirement describes list filtering, not inline completion. It does not cover a combobox that must allow free-text values that are *not* in the list as a final committed value beyond what the user typed (covered lightly under "Tab to exit"). It does not cover server-side relevance ranking quality — only its a11y announcement. --- ## Semantic Structure Plan **Reference pattern:** WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide — **Combobox Pattern**: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/ and the editable combobox with list autocomplete example: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/examples/combobox-autocomplete-list/ **Landmark context:** The combobox itself is a widget, not a landmark. When used as a site/section search, the host page should wrap it in a `` landmark (the HTML `search` element / `role="search"`) so screen reader users can jump to it via landmark navigation (WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks). The component should NOT hard-code the landmark itself (it is reusable in non-search contexts like a form field); instead it exposes a documented slot so the host decides. Document this in the component README. **Heading hierarchy:** The combobox introduces no headings. Group labels inside the listbox are NOT headings — they are non-interactive group labels (see grouping below), because a listbox's accessible children are options and group containers, not heading structure. **Critical structural rule — the input is a sibling of the listbox, not a parent.** In the current (ARIA 1.2) combobox pattern, `role="combobox"` goes on the `` itself, and the popup listbox is a **separate element** referenced by `aria-controls`. The input must NOT contain the listbox. A wrapper element groups them for layout and for the `aria-controls` relationship. **Semantic elements:** | Region | Element | Why | WCAG | |---|---|---|---| | Wrapper | `
` (presentational) | Layout + relationship container; not announced | — | | Visible label | `

_benchmark.method: plan 006 Phase D Claude lane: Agent(subagent_type=a11y-planner, model=opus), one background subagent per fixture; verbatim PLANNER_PROMPT_PREFIX task; agent wrote plan via Write tool; orchestrator wrapped to response JSON
